grammar.description: Grounds the affirmation in a completed past action or state — celebrating past strength and achievement.
grammar.name: ✅ Finite Indicative Active Past Simple First Singular
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time.
- Use the past tense.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I was a brave explorer navigating the unknown
I am ready to move forward without hesitation
I am the traveler who sails toward a better future

grammar.description: Asserts a present-moment truth directly and simply — the most fundamental form of affirmation.
grammar.name: ⭐ Finite Indicative Active Present Simple First Singular
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time.
- Use the present tense.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I am an adventurer who drives life forward
I step out of my comfort zone to find fulfillment
I embrace the unknown to grow and thrive

grammar.description: Projects confident intention toward the future — affirming what one will be or achieve.
grammar.name: ⏭️ Finite Indicative Active Future Simple First Singular
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time.
- Use the future tense with 'will'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I will step out of my comfort zone to explore new horizons
I will trust that the journey of life brings fulfillment
I will navigate my path with adaptability and clarity

grammar.description: Affirms continuous progress that began in the past and is still unfolding — capturing momentum and sustained effort.
grammar.name: 🔄 Finite Indicative Active Present Perfect Progressive First Singular
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Use the perfect progressive: 'I have been [verb]ing...'. The sentence must open with 'I have been'. Coordinated verbs must also use the -ing form, e.g., 'I have been growing and becoming...'
- Use the present tense.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I have been navigating life's waters with open arms and a firm sail
I have been stepping out of static patterns to embrace the unknown with courage
I have been driving forward through challenges to unlock new possibilities and find purpose

grammar.description: Visualizes a future moment where something is already accomplished — affirming from the vantage point of completion.
grammar.name: 🎯 Finite Indicative Active Future Perfect Perfective First Singular
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action as a complete, bounded whole — viewed start-to-finish.
- Use FUTURE PERFECT tense: 'I will have [past participle]...'. This is DIFFERENT from present perfect ('I have [past participle]'). 'I will have become...', 'I will have achieved...', 'I will have created...' are all valid future perfect. The 'will have' structure is mandatory.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I will have sailed toward a new destination
I will have become an explorer in this moment
I will have created abundance in this water

grammar.description: Affirms shared strength and collective identity — embracing connection and belonging with others.
grammar.name: 🤝 Finite Indicative Active Present Simple First Plural
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time.
- Use the present tense.
- Subject is 'we' (first person plural).

affirmations.text: We stand together as one true ship of destiny
We walk forward as a single vessel for our purpose
We move boldly as the captain of our shared journey

grammar.description: Speaks a present truth about another person — broadening perspective beyond the self.
grammar.name: 👤 Finite Indicative Active Present Simple Third
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time.
- Use the present tense.
- CRITICAL: Do NOT use 'I'. The subject must be third-person: 'he', 'she', 'it', or 'they'. Never start the sentence with 'I'.

affirmations.text: They explore new horizons without feeling trapped
She takes a bold step toward a new beginning
He navigates life with clarity and purpose

grammar.description: Affirms capability and possibility — what the speaker can do or is able to accomplish.
grammar.name: 💪 Finite Potential Active Present Simple First Singular
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- CRITICAL: The main verb structure MUST use 'can' or 'could' or 'able to': 'I can...', 'I could...', 'I am able to...'. Do NOT use indicative verbs like 'trust', 'believe', or 'know' without 'can'/'could'.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time.
- Use the present tense.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I am able to find new horizons through freedom
I can embrace the unknown to unlock possibilities
I am ready to move forward with adaptability

grammar.description: Expresses a sincere wish or deep longing — voicing the heart's desire with openness and hope.
grammar.name: 🙏 Finite Optative Active First Singular
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- Express a wish or hope.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: May I travel freely without being trapped by the sea
If only I could sail into the unknown to find my path
Would that I could move forward to explore new horizons?

grammar.description: Commands the self or another to act — activating, direct, and rousing.
grammar.name: ❗ Finite Imperative Active Second Singular
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- Use the imperative mood: start the sentence with a bare infinitive verb — NO explicit subject. The implicit (unstated) subject is always 'you'. Subordinate clauses in the same sentence may use any voice, tense, or person — only the MAIN verb (the one that starts the sentence) must be bare imperative. Do NOT write 'You should...', 'You must...', or any sentence that starts with 'I'.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Subject/address is 'you' (second person).

affirmations.text: You move forward without bound!
You build your ship to travel
You embrace the unknown to flourish

grammar.description: Challenges limiting beliefs through a rhetorical question — confronting self-doubt head-on.
grammar.name: ❓ Finite Interrogative Active Present First Singular Negative
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- CRITICAL: The sentence MUST be a QUESTION and MUST end with '?'. Required patterns: 'Am I not...?', 'Have I not...?', 'Can I not...?', 'Do I not...?'. The '?' at the very end of the sentence is MANDATORY — never omit it. Do NOT write a declarative statement. 'Can' and 'have' are valid finite auxiliaries.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Include negation using 'not', 'never', 'no longer', or 'without'.
- Use the present tense.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I am not afraid of the unknown
I have not stopped dreaming of new horizons
Can I not leave the comfort zone to find my true self?

grammar.description: Names a purpose or aspiration in its purest infinitive form — to be, to do, to become.
grammar.name: ♾️ Infinitive Active Simple
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Start with the base infinitive: 'To [verb]...' — no subject or conjugation.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time.

affirmations.text: To be an explorer who leaves the comfort zone to embrace the unknown
To travel forward without being trapped by the current path
To navigate life's waters with adaptability and fluidity

grammar.description: Affirms through process and action — celebrating the doing and becoming, not merely the state of being.
grammar.name: 💡 Gerund Active
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Begin with a gerund (-ing form as a noun).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.

affirmations.text: I choose to walk the unknown water daily, embracing freedom
I transform my static patterns into fluid action to find purpose
I step out of my comfort zone to explore a life of abundance

grammar.description: Affirms being received, held, or supported — openness to grace, love, and forces beyond the self.
grammar.name: 🌊 Finite Indicative Passive Present Simple First Singular
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- CRITICAL: The MAIN clause must use PASSIVE voice — the subject receives the action. Pattern: 'I am/was/have been [past participle]'. Subordinate clauses and infinitive phrases (e.g., 'ready to feel', 'able to see') may use active voice. Do NOT write active main clauses like 'I trust', 'I choose', or 'I feel'.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time.
- Use the present tense.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I am ready to embrace the unknown in my waters
I am guided by the strength of my ship to find my path
I walk forward with freedom, letting the ocean take my shape

grammar.description: Reinforces a repeated practice or pattern — affirming what the speaker consistently does or believes.
grammar.name: 🔁 Finite Indicative Active Present Habitual First Singular
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express a recurring action using 'always', 'regularly', 'consistently', or 'every day'.
- Use the present tense.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I consistently step out of my comfort zone to find new possibilities
I regularly embrace the unknown to unlock new horizons of life
I always move forward without getting trapped, guided by the path of the ship

grammar.description: Affirms by negating a false belief or limitation — releasing what is not true about the self.
grammar.name: 🫷 Finite Indicative Active Present Simple First Singular Negative
grammar.specifier_descriptions:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Include negation using 'not', 'never', 'no longer', or 'without'.
- Express the action plainly without specifying duration or extent over time.
- Use the present tense.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I am not trapped by the static structure of my old habits
I am not bound by the comfort of the familiar routine
I do not need to stop moving to find my true path
